title: Request Construction, Method Semantics, Safety, and Idempotency · Browser and Node Client Systems · Type-System Trace: Request Components
summary: A typed deployment console creates jobs from repeated clicks and stale tabs, requiring method semantics, preconditions, and duplicate-effect controls. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http ts request components increment in a TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch method-aware request builder. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Construct a request with an intentional method, target, fields, body, context, and caller-owned policy. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Canonicalize and validate schemes, hosts, IP literals, ports, IDNs, and resolved destinations before granting network authority. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
